mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 7.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 7.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces forward earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 102.0 (index 2017=100) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO > 102.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to expected Federal Reserve tightening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline occur simultaneously, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 265.0 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both CPIAUCSL > 265.0 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency signals the strength of aggregate demand; sustained growth above trend produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances during periods of positive GDPC1 growth, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1300 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as a leading indicator of economic slowdown.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1300, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
